5. Also check if `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` has a `FunctionInvokingChatClient` or similar wrapper that handles the tool-call loop automatically.

Report all findings with namespace and class names.

<member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient">
            <summary>
            A delegating chat client that invokes functions defined on <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions"/>.
            Include this in a chat pipeline to resolve function calls automatically.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            When this client receives a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/> in a chat response from its inner
            <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/>, it responds by invoking the corresponding <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> defined
            in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Tools"/> (or in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AdditionalTools"/>), producing a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionResultContent"/>
            that it sends back to the inner client. This loop is repeated until there are no more function calls to make, or until
            another stop condition is met, such as hitting <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.MaximumIterationsPerRequest"/>.
            </para>
            <para>
            If a requested function is an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration"/> but not an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>, the
            <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> will not attempt to invoke it, and instead allow that <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/>
<exited with exit code 0>

================================================================================
3. KEY APIs FOR SPECIFIC TASKS
================================================================================

--- CREATING AN IChatClient FROM OpenAI ---

Method 1: Direct wrapping of OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient
  var openAiClient = new ChatClient("gpt-4", apiKey);
  var chatClient = openAiClient.AsIChatClient();

Method 2: Using OpenAIChatClient constructor
  var openAiClient = new ChatClient("gpt-4", apiKey);
  IChatClient chatClient = new OpenAIChatClient(openAiClient);

Method 3: From AssistantClient
  var assistantClient = new AssistantClient(apiKey);
  IChatClient chatClient = assistantClient.AsIChatClient(
    assistantId: "asst_...",
    threadId: "thread_..." // optional
  );

Method 4: Using ChatClientBuilder
  var chatClient = new ChatClientBuilder()
    .Use(openAiClient.AsIChatClient())
    .Build(serviceProvider);

--- DEFINING TOOLS/FUNCTIONS ---

Method 1: Using AIFunctionFactory with [Description] attributes
  public class Tools
  {
    [Description("Gets the current temperature")]
    public static async Task<string> GetWeather(
      [Description("City name")] string city,
      [Description("Unit (C or F)")] string unit = "C")
    {
      return $"The weather in {city} is...";
    }
  }
  
  var function = AIFunctionFactory.Create(
    typeof(Tools).GetMethod("GetWeather")
  );

Method 2: Manual AIFunction creation
  var function = new AIFunction()
  {
    // Set properties manually
  };

Method 3: Using AITool (metadata only, no invocation)
  var tool = new AITool()
  {
    Name = "get_weather",
    Description = "Gets the current weather"
  };

--- TOOL-CALL LOOP / AGENT LOOP ---

Automatic Loop (FunctionInvokingChatClient):
  var openAi = new ChatClient("gpt-4", apiKey).AsIChatClient();
  
  // Wrap with FunctionInvokingChatClient
  var toolClient = new ChatClientBuilder()
    .Use(openAi)
    .UseFunctionInvocation(loggerFactory)
    .Build(serviceProvider);
  
  // Now when LLM calls functions, they're auto-invoked and results sent back
  var response = await toolClient.GetResponseAsync(
    messages,
    new ChatOptions
    {
      Tools = new[] { /* AIFunction definitions */ },
      MaximumIterationsPerRequest = 10  // limit iterations
    }
  );
  
  // Result contains final LLM response after all tool calls resolved

Manual Loop (ChatClientAgent):
  var agent = new ChatClientAgent(
    chatClient,
    options,
    loggerFactory,
    serviceProvider
  );
  
  // Run agent (handles tool calls internally)
  var response = await agent.RunAsync(
    messages,
    new AgentRunOptions()
  );
  
  // Agent automatically invokes tools and continues loop

Agent Pipeline (AIAgentBuilder):
  var agent = new AIAgentBuilder(baseAgent)
    .Use(agentMiddleware1)
    .Use(agentMiddleware2)
    .UseAIContextProviders(contextProviders)
    .Build(serviceProvider);
  
  var response = await agent.RunAsync(messages, options);

--- FUNCTION INVOCATION OPTIONS ---

ChatOptions properties for tools:
  • Tools: ICollection<AITool> - set of available tools
  • ToolMode: ChatToolMode - "auto" | "required" | "none"
  • AllowMultipleToolCalls: bool - allow parallel tool calls

FunctionInvokingChatClient properties:
  • MaximumIterationsPerRequest: int - max tool-call iterations (default 10)
  • AdditionalTools: ICollection<AIFunction> - additional tools to invoke

--- AIFunctionFactory.Create() SIGNATURES ---

Create overloads available:
  • Create(method) -> AIFunction
  • Create(method, target) -> AIFunction
  • Create(method, target, options) -> AIFunction
  • Create(method, target, options, invocationHandler) -> AIFunction

Parameters:
  • method: MethodInfo - the .NET method to wrap
  • target: object (optional) - instance for instance methods
  • options: AIFunctionFactoryOptions - configuration
  • invocationHandler: Func<...> (optional) - custom invocation logic

AIFunctionFactoryOptions:
  • ParameterBindingOptions - control parameter binding behavior
  
Attribute Support:
  • [Description("...")] - used on method and parameters
  • Automatically generates JSON schema from method signature

================================================================================
4. AGENT ARCHITECTURE & EXECUTION FLOW
================================================================================

Agent Execution Loop (ChatClientAgent):

  1. User calls agent.RunAsync(messages, options)
  
  2. Agent creates or uses existing AgentSession
  
  3. Calls RunCoreAsync() which:
     a. Notifies AIContextProviders to prepare context
     b. Builds full chat message history with context
     c. Calls underlying ChatClient with messages + tools
     
  4. If LLM response contains FunctionCallContent:
     a. Invokes the function using AIFunction.InvokeAsync()
     b. Creates FunctionResultContent with result
     c. Adds both to message history
     d. Sends back to LLM (continues loop)
     
  5. When LLM returns text response (no more tool calls):
     a. Notifies providers of new messages
     b. Returns AgentResponse to caller
     
  6. On error:
     a. Notifies providers via NotifyProvidersOfFailureAsync()
     b. Propagates exception

Agent Session Management:
  • Sessions maintain chat history across multiple RunAsync calls
  • Can be serialized/deserialized for persistence
  • CreateSessionAsync() creates new session
  • SerializeSessionCoreAsync() for persistence
  • DeserializeSessionCoreAsync() for restoration

Agent Middleware Pipeline (AIAgentBuilder):
  
  The AIAgentBuilder.Use() method creates middleware chains:
  
  Request flow:
    User Input
        ↓
    [Middleware 1] (e.g., LoggingAgent)
        ↓
    [Middleware 2] (e.g., FunctionInvocationDelegatingAgent)
        ↓
    [Base Agent] (e.g., ChatClientAgent)
        ↓
    [LLM Service]
        ↓
    Response flows back up the chain

Each middleware can:
  • Modify messages before passing down
  • Intercept responses before passing up
  • Add/remove tools
  • Log/monitor calls
  • Apply transformations

================================================================================
3. KEY APIs FOR SPECIFIC TASKS
================================================================================

--- CREATING AN IChatClient FROM OpenAI ---

Method 1: Direct wrapping of OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient
  var openAiClient = new ChatClient("gpt-4", apiKey);
  var chatClient = openAiClient.AsIChatClient();

Method 2: Using OpenAIChatClient constructor
  var openAiClient = new ChatClient("gpt-4", apiKey);
  IChatClient chatClient = new OpenAIChatClient(openAiClient);

Method 3: From AssistantClient
  var assistantClient = new AssistantClient(apiKey);
  IChatClient chatClient = assistantClient.AsIChatClient(
    assistantId: "asst_...",
    threadId: "thread_..." // optional
  );

Method 4: Using ChatClientBuilder
  var chatClient = new ChatClientBuilder()
    .Use(openAiClient.AsIChatClient())
    .Build(serviceProvider);

--- DEFINING TOOLS/FUNCTIONS ---

Method 1: Using AIFunctionFactory with [Description] attributes
  public class Tools
  {
    [Description("Gets the current temperature")]
    public static async Task<string> GetWeather(
      [Description("City name")] string city,
      [Description("Unit (C or F)")] string unit = "C")
    {
      return $"The weather in {city} is...";
    }
  }
  
  var function = AIFunctionFactory.Create(
    typeof(Tools).GetMethod("GetWeather")
  );

Method 2: Manual AIFunction creation
  var function = new AIFunction()
  {
    // Set properties manually
  };

Method 3: Using AITool (metadata only, no invocation)
  var tool = new AITool()
  {
    Name = "get_weather",
    Description = "Gets the current weather"
  };

--- TOOL-CALL LOOP / AGENT LOOP ---

Automatic Loop (FunctionInvokingChatClient):
  var openAi = new ChatClient("gpt-4", apiKey).AsIChatClient();
  
  // Wrap with FunctionInvokingChatClient
  var toolClient = new ChatClientBuilder()
    .Use(openAi)
    .UseFunctionInvocation(loggerFactory)
    .Build(serviceProvider);
  
  // Now when LLM calls functions, they're auto-invoked and results sent back
  var response = await toolClient.GetResponseAsync(
    messages,
    new ChatOptions
    {
      Tools = new[] { /* AIFunction definitions */ },
      MaximumIterationsPerRequest = 10  // limit iterations
    }
  );
  
  // Result contains final LLM response after all tool calls resolved

Manual Loop (ChatClientAgent):
  var agent = new ChatClientAgent(
    chatClient,
    options,
    loggerFactory,
    serviceProvider
  );
  
  // Run agent (handles tool calls internally)
  var response = await agent.RunAsync(
    messages,
    new AgentRunOptions()
  );
  
  // Agent automatically invokes tools and continues loop

Agent Pipeline (AIAgentBuilder):
  var agent = new AIAgentBuilder(baseAgent)
    .Use(agentMiddleware1)
    .Use(agentMiddleware2)
    .UseAIContextProviders(contextProviders)
    .Build(serviceProvider);
  
  var response = await agent.RunAsync(messages, options);

--- FUNCTION INVOCATION OPTIONS ---

ChatOptions properties for tools:
  • Tools: ICollection<AITool> - set of available tools
  • ToolMode: ChatToolMode - "auto" | "required" | "none"
  • AllowMultipleToolCalls: bool - allow parallel tool calls

FunctionInvokingChatClient properties:
  • MaximumIterationsPerRequest: int - max tool-call iterations (default 10)
  • AdditionalTools: ICollection<AIFunction> - additional tools to invoke

--- AIFunctionFactory.Create() SIGNATURES ---

Create overloads available:
  • Create(method) -> AIFunction
  • Create(method, target) -> AIFunction
  • Create(method, target, options) -> AIFunction
  • Create(method, target, options, invocationHandler) -> AIFunction

Parameters:
  • method: MethodInfo - the .NET method to wrap
  • target: object (optional) - instance for instance methods
  • options: AIFunctionFactoryOptions - configuration
  • invocationHandler: Func<...> (optional) - custom invocation logic

AIFunctionFactoryOptions:
  • ParameterBindingOptions - control parameter binding behavior
  
Attribute Support:
  • [Description("...")] - used on method and parameters
  • Automatically generates JSON schema from method signature

================================================================================
4. AGENT ARCHITECTURE & EXECUTION FLOW
================================================================================

Agent Execution Loop (ChatClientAgent):

  1. User calls agent.RunAsync(messages, options)
  
  2. Agent creates or uses existing AgentSession
  
  3. Calls RunCoreAsync() which:
     a. Notifies AIContextProviders to prepare context
     b. Builds full chat message history with context
     c. Calls underlying ChatClient with messages + tools
     
  4. If LLM response contains FunctionCallContent:
     a. Invokes the function using AIFunction.InvokeAsync()
     b. Creates FunctionResultContent with result
     c. Adds both to message history
     d. Sends back to LLM (continues loop)
     
  5. When LLM returns text response (no more tool calls):
     a. Notifies providers of new messages
     b. Returns AgentResponse to caller
     
  6. On error:
     a. Notifies providers via NotifyProvidersOfFailureAsync()
     b. Propagates exception

Agent Session Management:
  • Sessions maintain chat history across multiple RunAsync calls
  • Can be serialized/deserialized for persistence
  • CreateSessionAsync() creates new session
  • SerializeSessionCoreAsync() for persistence
  • DeserializeSessionCoreAsync() for restoration

Agent Middleware Pipeline (AIAgentBuilder):
  
  The AIAgentBuilder.Use() method creates middleware chains:
  
  Request flow:
    User Input
        ↓
    [Middleware 1] (e.g., LoggingAgent)
        ↓
    [Middleware 2] (e.g., FunctionInvocationDelegatingAgent)
        ↓
    [Base Agent] (e.g., ChatClientAgent)
        ↓
    [LLM Service]
        ↓
    Response flows back up the chain

Each middleware can:
  • Modify messages before passing down
  • Intercept responses before passing up
  • Add/remove tools
  • Log/monitor calls
  • Apply transformations

================================================================================
5. FEATURES & CAPABILITIES SUMMARY
================================================================================

Core Features:
  ✓ Multiple LLM backends (OpenAI Chat, Assistants, Responses, Realtime)
  ✓ Automatic tool-call loop (FunctionInvokingChatClient)
  ✓ Function reflection & schema generation (AIFunctionFactory)
  ✓ Streaming responses (GetStreamingResponseAsync)
  ✓ Agent sessions with history management
  ✓ Agent middleware pipeline (AIAgentBuilder)
  ✓ Context injection (AIContextProvider)
  ✓ Structured output (response formats)
  ✓ Function caching
  ✓ Agent evaluation framework

Tool Management:
  ✓ Auto-invoke from method reflection
  ✓ JSON schema generation from parameters
  ✓ [Description] attribute support
  ✓ Type parameter conversion
  ✓ Custom parameter binding

Conversation Management:
  ✓ Chat history memory providers
  ✓ Message compaction strategies (sliding window, truncation, summarization)
  ✓ Conversation splitting
  ✓ Hosted file integration

Observability:
  ✓ Logging via ILoggerFactory
  ✓ OpenTelemetry support (OpenTelemetryAgent)
  ✓ Custom evaluation framework

data.content:
Everything works. The app builds cleanly and exits gracefully when no API key is set.

Here's what the app does:

**Architecture:**
- Uses `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` with `FunctionInvokingChatClient` to automatically handle the agent tool-call loop
- Connects to OpenAI via the `OpenAI` SDK, wrapped as an `IChatClient`

**Three tools exposed to the agent:**
| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `SearchWeb` | Queries DuckDuckGo and extracts result snippets |
| `TakeNote` | Saves titled notes to an in-memory list |
| `GetNotes` | Retrieves all saved notes for review |

**Agent workflow:** The system prompt instructs the LLM to search ≥3 queries → take notes on findings → review notes → produce a structured summary. `FunctionInvokingChatClient` handles the multi-turn tool-call loop automatically.

**Usage:**
```bash
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."
dotnet run -- "quantum computing"       # topic via args
dotnet run                              # interactive prompt
export OPENAI_MODEL="gpt-4o-mini"       # optional model override
```
